label: Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families)

node.label: Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families)
node.definition: A policy is adjusted toward preferred behavior while controlling deviation from a reference model.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is modeled as depending on Preference learning, Reward modeling and feeding downstream nodes such as Direct and online preference optimization, Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
examples: atlas downstream link to Direct and online preference optimization
atlas downstream link to Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment
atlas downstream link to Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Reward modeling.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) has a `uses` dependency on Preference learning because Preference learning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Reward modeling because Reward modeling is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
In this atlas graph, Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
